sourceMovie.title: The Ugly Truth
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Ugly Truth for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Robert Luketic keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pretty Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ugly Truth clicked for you, Pretty Woman (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Ugly Truth?
faq.answer: Try Pretty Woman, There's Something About Mary, Set It Up—each shares DNA with The Ugly Truth while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Ugly Truth?
faq.answer: The Ugly Truth (2009) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Ugly Truth worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
